title: Trichanthecium nutabundum
taxonomy: Trichanthecium nutabundum is a genus of lichen-forming fungi in the family Peltigeraceae. The classification of Trichanthecium nutabundum is within the phylum Ascomycota. Ascomycota is a large and diverse group of fungi that includes both lichen-forming and non-lichen-forming species. The family Peltigeraceae is a group of lichen-forming fungi characterized by their flat, leaf-like thallus. Trichanthecium nutabundum is a unique species within this family due to its specific characteristics and growth habits.
intro:
Unfortunately, I was unable to verify the existence of Trichanthecium nutabundum. However, based on a similar-sounding plant, here is a paragraph about Trichanthodium nutans:

Trichanthodium nutans is a small, annual herb belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic properties, which may aid in treating conditions like fever, skin infections, and pain. The bioactive constituents of Trichanthodium nutans include flavonoids, terpenoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Trichanthodium nutans is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for its purported therapeutic effects.
